observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Accountant –
a. Trace and reconcile the discrepancy between balances per SL,
PPELC, and IIRUP;
b. Prepare adjusting entries for tangible items with costs below
₱50,000.00 that are still recognized as PPE; and
c. Henceforth, ensure that transactions are properly recorded in the
books in accordance with the prescribed accounting standards, rules,
and regulations.
2. The GSO Designate –
a. Prepare and submit the RPCPPE;
b. Prepare and maintain Property Cards.

We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management
conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the
guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the
accounting and property records, which have become a perennial issue and
caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented
in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to
record the collections made. Henceforth, ensure that all collections and
deposits are properly recorded in the books so as to present an accurate
account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant update the accounting
records to reflect the encashed checks as cleared to fairly present the balance
of Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the noted book
reconciling items and secure supporting documents, reconcile the adjusted
cash in bank balance in the BRS with the book balance, and submit the same
to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer –
a. Always monitor the cash in bank balances for all Funds; and
b. Stop certifying as to the availability of funds and refrain from drawing
checks if such funds are insufficient in the bank.

We also recommended that the LGU implement corrective measures to
ensure adequacy of cash, such as the enforcement of taxes or collections
of receivable due to the Municipality.

We recommended that the GSO Designate regularly conduct a physical count
of inventories every six months and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit
Team no later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semester, respectively.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the
disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents recorded as Welfare
Goods for Distribution and Welfare Goods Expense.

We recommended that the General Services Officer submit the lacking
requested copies of AIRs and delivery receipts for CY 2024.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer demand from the
supplier the issuance of Official Receipts, and the signature of the
workers/laborers in the payroll for the received wages, to establish the
legitimacy of the settlement of payables and receipt of payment by the
suppliers and workers/laborers, and submit the same to the audit team.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant only record
obligations as Accounts Payable if it meet the definition; otherwise, record it
to a more appropriate liability account.

2022, We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity:
23. Properly monitor the funds received by the source agency for funds transferred to the Municipality, by identifying the intended purpose of each grant, and appropriately recording the same in the books; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following responsible officials:
A. Municipal Treasurer to:
32. Submit regularly the monthly Report of Collections and Deposits, Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value for all funds issued to the Audit Team; remit/transfer regularly SEF collections to the Cash in Local Treasury account of SEF per Subsidiary Ledger; 33. Remit/ transfer regularly SEF collection to the Cash in Local Treasury account of SEF per Subsidiary Ledger; B. Municipal Accountant to:
34. Have a regular reconciliation between Collectors’, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant’s records in the recording of collections and deposits and other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be made on a monthly basis and to substantiate the reported Cash in Local Treasury account in the financial statement.
Not Implemented
observations.reason: Collections were still not regularly recorded; reconciliation not regularly conducted due to non-submission of reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
36. Municipal Accountant to: (i) coordinate with the GSO Designate to reconstruct and reconcile their records, taking into consideration the accuracy of the beginning inventory balances, the purchases during the year that were recorded as inventory during the year that were recorded as inventory that were reclassified to expenses, the actual issuances, and other related adjustments, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and (ii) coordinate with the Inventory Committee and the GSO Designate to obtain the balance of inventory per physical count at the end of the year, reconcile balances and prepare adjusting entries, if warranted.
37. GSO Designate to reconcile property records with the result of annual physical count and accounting records.
38. Inventory Committee to: (i) submit complete RPCI; and (ii) regularly conduct physical count of inventories and submit a copy of the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
Not Implemented
observations.reason: No physical count conducted; RPCI not submitted; records were not reconciled.

45. Ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by the Management are duly accomplished and include all the basic information as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010 to protect the interest of the Municipal Government.
46. Establish an internal control mechanism that would ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by Management are duly accomplished and include all the basic information as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010 to protect the interest of the Municipal Government.
Not Implemented

Not Implemented
observations.reason: No justification submitted.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Municipal Accountant –
a. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the above-mentioned accounting
errors and deficiencies, for proper valuation of the PPE;
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the status of completion
of the projects; and
c. Henceforth, ensure that transactions are properly recorded in the
books, in accordance with the prescribed accounting standards, rules,
and regulations.
2. The GSO Designate –
a. Prepare and submit the RPCPPE;
b. Conduct reconciliation of the PPE accounts between the accounting
and property records; and
c. Prepare and maintain Property Cards.
3. The Chairperson of the Inventory Committee informs the Municipal
Accountant and the GSO of the result of the physical count of PPE, to
update their respective records and facilitate the preparation of reports.
We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management
conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines
and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020,
to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the accounting and
property records, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-
establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial
statements.

2.27 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Draw the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted errors after
verification and supported with documents;
b. Reconcile the balances between the ledgers, journals, trial balance, and
financial statements; and
c. Thoroughly review the accounts and observe the proper recording of
transactions in the books.
2.28 The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting
journal entries to recognize the unliquidated cash advances under the Social
Pension to Indigent Senior Citizens as of December 31, 2023. Henceforth,
observe the proper recording of cash advance transactions in the books.
3.6 The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the audit finding and committed to draw
adjustments in the books.

4.10 We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Treasurer –
a. Always monitor the cash in bank balances for all Funds; and
b. Stop certifying as to the availability of funds, and refrain from drawing
checks, if such funds are insufficient in the bank.
2. The Municipal Accountant –
a. Prepare the bank reconciliation statements monthly, to regularly
monitor the cash in bank balances and regulate disbursements; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of accounting records with the
Municipal Treasurer’s records.
4.11 During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer informed the audit team that
funds from the GF LBP account has already been transferred to the GF DBP account
to cover the obligations relative to the outstanding checks in CY 2023.
4.12 The Municipal Accountant agreed to follow the recommendations.

5.11 We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Treasurer:
a. Require the AOs to remit all receipts/collections daily and intact to the
Municipal Treasurer, and refrain from giving an additional series of
accountable forms to the AOs without complete remittance of collections
and turnover of copies of the previously issued ORs;
b. Direct the AOs to stop the practice of issuing official receipts without the
actual receipt of cash;
c. Strictly monitor the requisitions of accountable forms, and ensure that
collecting officers are not granted unrestricted access to them; and
d. Install strict control measures to safeguard government funds from loss or
misuse.
5.12 The Municipal Treasurer informed the audit team that daily and intact remittances of
collections is already being strictly enforced in CY 2024. She added that the
requisitions of the accountable forms are also strictly monitored by her, and
additional series is not issued to AOs unless the cash collections from the previously
issued ORs are duly remitted to her.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the collecting officers
strictly monitor the effectivity period of their respective fidelity bond and ensure
that renewal thereof be made prior to its expiration.
6.9 The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the audit findings and committed to comply
with the recommendation.

7.12 We recommended that the:
a. Local Budget Officer updates the RAAO regularly and verifies the
balances of appropriations before certifying the existence of available
appropriations in the Certification on Appropriations, Funds, and Obligations
of Allotment, and limit the disbursement of funds to what has been duly
appropriated in the annual budget.
b. Local Chief Executive and the Sanggunian Bayan determine the presence of
the required conditions before realignment or enactment of a Supplemental
Budget, through an Ordinance, such as, funds are available, generating a new
revenue source, or state declaration of public calamity.
7.13 The Local Chief Executive and the Budget Officer agreed to comply with the
recommendations.

8.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian Bayan
refrain from approving the reprogramming of funds from LDRRMF-CO to
other item classes to ascertain the propriety and validity of the changes made to
the budget.
8.11 The Local Chief Executive agreed to comply with the recommendation.

9.13 We recommended that the LGU:
a. Obtain a certification from the identified beneficiaries, showing the same
signatures as presented in their respective valid IDs, to prove that they
have indeed received the stipend for the 1st and 2nd Semesters of CY 2023;
and
b. Submit the identified lacking documents to the Auditor and DSWD FO
VIII.
9.14 Henceforth, we recommended that the SDO and the focal person of the social
pension program:
a. Verify the validity of the signatures on the payroll and that appearing on
the presented valid ID; and
b. Check the completeness of documents before giving the stipend to the
beneficiaries/authorized representative of beneficiaries.
9.15 During the exit conference, the MSWDO explained that they have been instructed
that the deceased beneficiaries were still entitled to the full amount of the stipend for
the semester if they died within the semester, following the reverted semestral
payouts in CY 2023. But they will send an inquiry to the DSWD, to clarify the
amounts that the deceased beneficiaries are entitled to, whether it be for the quarter
only or for the semester, as regards the period of death, following the amendments in
the DSWD Memorandum Circulars for Social Pension Program for Indigent Senior
Citizens.

10.5 We recommended that the LGU comply with the stipulations in the Memoranda
of Agreement executed with the source agencies for all funds received for the
implementation of projects/programs/activities and undertake liquidation
thereof within the agreed period.
10.6 The officials concerned agreed to comply with the recommendation.

11.12. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that all
the provisions of the Philippine Bidding Documents are complied with in the
conduct of procurement of infrastructure projects, such that all required
bidding documents are prepared and issued to the bidders and subsequently
included in the Contract Agreement.
11.13. The BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat Head agreed to follow the
recommendation.

observations.reason: Delivery Receipt and Summary List of Affected Households/Families not submitted initially; later submitted. Delivery Receipt and Distribution List in date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 31. Submit the RAAOs, relative to LDRRMF transactions, for audit and verification.
Fully Implemented

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to strictly follow the general procedures of disposing a property or asset in order to correctly conduct and determine the best mode of disposal. We further recommend the Municipal Accountant to properly support the derecognition of an asset or property.
Implemented

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer to thoroughly review the compliance of the Annual Budget to the limitations set by existing laws and regulations, in this case the threshold for discretionary fund or EME.
Fully Implemented

We recommended that the Accounting Office of the Municipality:
40. Submit the Requisition and Issue Slip and Distribution List in relation to the issuance of the relief goods mentioned,
41. Submit the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for the issuance of the communication equipment to its user,
42. Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors presented.
Implemented

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the unsubmitted contracts for evaluation and review. Further, we recommended to strictly comply with the provisions in COA Circular 2009-001 which provides that contracts shall be submitted within five (5) working days from its execution supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated in the circular.
Fully Implemented

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials in the custody of responsible officials and determine unused or undistributed items. Likewise, require the submission of Stock Cards, Requisition Issue Slip, Inventory Custodian Slip and such other records/reports supporting issuance of supplies and materials.
Fully Implemented

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to cause proper monitoring and evaluation of project implementation and accomplishment to ensure that government funds are optimally utilized and benefits are received by the intended beneficiaries. Prospectively, report on the status of the projects undertaken by the recipient agencies, supported with accomplishment reports for evaluation.
Fully Implemented

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to properly reconcile the records and reports for Property, Plant and Equipment as provided in the above cited circular. Also, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain separate ledgers for Accumulated Depreciation for each property to accurately post the amount of cost and depreciation in Trial Balance.
observations.managementAction: Properly reconcile the records and reports for Property, Plant and Equipment as provided in the above cited circular. observations.reason: The RPCPPE was not submitted to the Audit Team. Also, the Municipal Accountant still do not prepare and maintain separate ledgers for the Accumulated Depreciation of each property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit the unsubmitted contract documents for evaluation and review and strictly comply with COA Circular 2009-001 which provides that contracts shall be submitted within five (5) working days from its execution supported with complete documentary requirements as enumerated in the circular.
observations.managementAction: Submit the unsubmitted contracts and supporting documents to COA for review and evaluation.
observations.reason: Contracts for Construction of Brgy. Hall of Brgy Garden (Phase I), Construction of Concrete Canal Cover remained unsubmitted as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials in the custody of responsible officials and determine unused or undistributed items. observations.managementAction: Conduct physical inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials in the custody of responsible officials and require submission of Stock Cards, RIS, ICS and other supporting records.
observations.reason: No RPCI submitted as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to prepare an updated Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System using the format prescribed on Annex C of the above cited circular.
observations.managementAction: Prepare an updated Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System using the prescribed format.
observations.reason: No submitted Report on Physical Count of the Road Network for CY 2022 as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to submit justification why the LGU failed to observe the required process of preparing a supplemental AIP to be approved by the local sanggunian before the funding requirement is made thru a resolution passed by the Sanggunian Bayan and approved by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: Submit justification why the LGU failed to observe the required process of preparing a supplemental AIP and ensure compliance prospectively.
observations.reason: No justification submitted to the Audit Team for the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to cause proper monitoring and evaluation of project implementation and ensure that government funds are optimally utilized and benefits are received by the intended beneficiaries. Prospectively, report on the status of the projects undertaken by the recipient agencies, supported with accomplishment report and other documents should be submitted to COA for evaluation. We also recommended that the LGU require the concerned recipient agency to submit Accomplishment Report for the assistance received before a new one will be granted in adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to monitor and evaluate project implementation and require submission of accomplishment reports and other supporting documents and require recipient agencies to submit Accomplishment Reports prior to granting new assistance.
observations.reason: No submitted Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team for the fund transfers made by the LGU to the barangays on CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare a separate RAO for QRF, MOOE and CO of the LDRRMF and ensure the realigned fund is properly incorporated in the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Prepare separate RAOs for QRF, MOOE and CO of the LDRRMF and incorporate the realigned fund in the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.reason: RAO were still not prepared separately for QRF, MOOE and CO.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Municipal Accountant –
a. Exert effort to trace back details of PPE acquisitions, and recompute
the accumulated depreciation as of CY 2022, taking into account the
appropriate residual value for each PPE, and the pertinent provisions
in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and PAG3. Thereafter, effect the necessary
adjustments upon determination of the correct amounts, for the proper
valuation of the PPE;
b. Maintain complete PPE ledger cards/subsidiary records for items of PPE
and coordinate with the GSO Designate/Inventory Committee for the
identification of recorded assets to be accounted in the physical count in
order to fast-track the preparation of the RPCPPE. Thereafter, undertake
the reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE
accounts in the financial statements.

tracing/identification of PPE to be accounted in the RPCPPE, and
expedite the preparation of the Report and submit the same to COA, to
comply with the provisions of NGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No.
2020-006;
b. Prepare and maintain complete Property Cards;
c. Communicate to the Municipal Accountant the result of the physical
count of PPE, to update his records.
We further recommended that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE
Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of unidentified PPE
items in the accounting records and discrepancies in PPE account balances, which
have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of
the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
1.21 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO Designate agreed to comply with the audit
recommendations.

We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Accountant –
a. Record adjustments for the restoration of unreleased checks at the end
of the year to the cash account, to fairly present the balances of the
Cash and Payable accounts at year-end;
b. Exert extra effort to identify the noted book reconciling items and
secure supporting documents, to reconcile the adjusted cash in bank
balance in the BRS with the book balance, and submit the same to the
Audit Team for verification; and
c. Record adjustments corresponding to the unrecorded receipts credited
directly to the HCI and PF Pooling accounts.
2. The Municipal Treasurer –
a. Prepare monthly the Report of Cancelled Checks, and submit the same
to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Ensure that the release of checks is properly acknowledged by the
payees in the Check Register, with dates of receipt indicated therein,
for monitoring; and
c. Prepare a list of unreleased checks at year end, and furnish the
Municipal Accountant, to serve as his basis for drawing adjustments.
2.20 The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the audit findings and assured that the
necessary adjusting entries will be drawn. Also, the Municipal Treasurer agreed to
comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entries to
correct the recording errors noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a duly certified list
showing the taxpayers and amounts due and collectible, and submit the same
to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year, to serve as the basis
for setting up the RPT and SET Receivables in the books.

We recommended that the management stops charging the prior year’s
expenses against the current year’s appropriations, unless with a supplemental
budget.
We further recommended that the officials concerned/Municipal Accountant
submit the distribution lists and other lacking documents, pertaining to the
above-mentioned transactions, and henceforth, thoroughly check the
completeness of supporting documents before processing claims, to avoid
suspensions in audit.

We recommended that the BAC submit the procurement contracts and
bidding documents for the above procurement of infrastructure projects and
strictly adhere to the timely submission of contracts and their supporting
documents within five working days from the execution thereof to facilitate
review and technical evaluation of the infrastructure projects.

supporting documents for the Repair of RHU building by the Municipal
Accountant. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and her Collectors apply the
correct tax rates on businesses.

Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts or Sales of the preceding calendar year
from the taxpayers, as the basis for the computation of business tax due,
pursuant to the Revenue Code of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

officials concerned, and henceforth, thoroughly check the completeness
of supporting documents of government transactions, before processing
of payments; and
b. Submit the disbursement voucher and supporting documents, relative to
the payment made for the procured emergency food packs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Properly monitor the funds received by the Municipality, by
maintaining separate records and supporting documents for each fund
transferred;
b. Coordinate with the Mayor’s Office or with the Municipal Treasurer,
regarding downloaded funds to the Municipality’s bank accounts from
NGAs, clearly identifying the intended purpose of each grant, and
appropriately recording the same in the books; and
c. Submit the lacking supporting documents, and strictly comply with the
documentary requirements for infrastructure projects, to avoid
suspensions in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer demand from the
contractor the issuance of an Official Receipt, for the deficient ₱500,000.00 in
the first progress payment.

We recommended that:

functions/duties, that there is no employer-employee relationship

copies of the logbook/biometric printouts, and Accomplishment Reports

period;

documents before claims are processed.
